mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 8.0 percent while nonfarm payrolls exceed 130,000 monthly additions, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the subsequent week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 8.0 and PAYEMS exceeds 130000
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative or flat in more than 50 percent of instances meeting the trigger condition across a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with increasing housing starts signals synchronized real-economy expansion, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no statistical difference between high and low INDPRO-HOUST co-movement periods (p > 0.10) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening monetary regime, a month-over-month consumer price index increase exceeding 0.3 percent forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero when CPI accelerates above the threshold during documented tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real retail and food sales volume directly reflects strengthening consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60 percent of periods following RSAFS increases over a 4-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index drops below 90.0, manufacturing capacity utilization falls into contraction territory, signaling weak aggregate demand and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are positive or show no significant decline when INDPRO breaches the 90.0 threshold across multiple recession episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth coupled with declining housing starts indicates a labor market shifting from cyclical recovery to structural overheating, which raises inflation expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns do not decline when PAYEMS rises above 150,000 and HOUST falls more than 5 percent sequentially over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery phase, a quarterly gross domestic product growth rate exceeding 3.0 percent alongside a declining unemployment rate confirms a V-shaped rebound, generating strongly positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth exceeds 3.0 percent
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or statistically flat when GDP growth exceeds 3.0 percent during the specified recovery phase across two consecutive quarters.
